signals.headline: SOL price stumbles to $79 (April 6 report), hitting April 2025 lows amid broader market risk-off from Fed rate hike sign
signals.raw:
SOL price stumbles to $79 (April 6 report), hitting April 2025 lows amid broader market risk-off from Fed rate hike signals, with Bitcoin at $66K[4][5].

No Bitcoin or general "April" events confirmed today; search results lack post-April 14 updates and show no prediction-market impacting resolutions[1][2][3][4][5][6]. **Confidence: HIGH** (based on absence in real-time results up to current times

signals.headline: **Alpenglow consensus upgrade** announced around April 14, targeting transaction finality reduction to 150 milliseconds,
signals.raw: **Alpenglow consensus upgrade** announced around April 14, targeting transaction finality reduction to 150 milliseconds, amid a record 167 million monthly token holders and 148 million daily non-vote transactions; this drove a 10% SOL surge but is not new today[1][3].

signals.headline: Lakers Defeat Rockets in Game 1 | CONFIRMED** | HIGH confidence
signals.raw:
Lakers Defeat Rockets in Game 1 | CONFIRMED** | HIGH confidence
The Los Angeles Lakers took Game 1 against the Houston Rockets[2], with Luke Kennard scoring 27 points and nailing five 3-pointers[3]. The Lakers have now extended to a 2-0 series lead with Marcus Smart's defense helping contain Kevin Durant in Game 2[3].

signals.headline: Cavaliers Defeat Raptors in First Round** | CONFIRMED | HIGH confidence
signals.raw:
Cavaliers Defeat Raptors in First Round** | CONFIRMED | HIGH confidence
The Cleveland Cavaliers crushed the Toronto Raptors 126-113 in Game 1[4], with Collin Sexton (Spida) scoring 32 points[4]. The Cavaliers won Game 2 as well, defeating Toronto 115-105[4], taking a commanding series lead.
matched_markets: NBA Playoffs: Who Will Win Series? - Raptors vs. Cavaliers

signals.headline: Victor Wembanyama's Dominant Playoff Debut** | CONFIRMED | HIGH confidence
signals.raw:
Victor Wembanyama's Dominant Playoff Debut** | CONFIRMED | HIGH confidence
Wembanyama scored 35 points in his playoff debut, a performance being discussed as historically significant[2].

signals.headline: **Drift Protocol $285M hack** on April 1 (attributed to North Korean actors), causing TVL drop from $9B to $5
signals.raw: **Drift Protocol $285M hack** on April 1 (attributed to North Korean actors), causing TVL drop from $9B to $5.5–6B, increased exchange deposits (1.4M SOL), and SOL price hovering at $78–82 with bearish indicators[2].
